purpose: Per-shape hexa-emit vs nvcc-cudaC SASS instruction count comparison for all 6 cookbook WMMA kernels. Extends a1f9d80b's step1-only nvcc_ref/sass_diff.txt (which measured step1 only at 1.000 ratio).

method.arch_source: matches hexa cookbook revalidate result.txt per-shape .target sm_NN (sm_80 for step1+step5, sm_90 for the rest)

shapes.description: Single warp, 16x16 WMMA, no K-loop. C[16x16] = A[16x16] * B[16x16] (f16xf16=f32).
shapes.honest_notes: Identical SASS count. Matches a1f9d80b's nvcc_ref/sass_diff.txt baseline exactly.

shapes.description: Single warp, WMMA K-loop, 4 K-tiles (K_TOTAL=64). C[16x16] = sum_k A[16x16] * B[16x16].
shapes.honest_notes: hexa emits 17.6% more SASS than nvcc. Likely K-loop unrolling differences: nvcc's NVVM may unroll the 4-iter loop more aggressively or fuse register allocation across iters; hexa cookbook PTX iterates explicitly. No functional difference suspected.

shapes.description: Multi-warp 64x64 grid: 512 threads = 16 warps, single block, each warp computes one 16x16 sub-tile via K-loop (4 K-tiles).
shapes.honest_notes: hexa is 19.2% leaner than nvcc here. nvcc's warp_id/m_tile/n_tile address-arithmetic may emit additional IMAD/IADD lanes for stride math; hexa's PTX folds these via constant block + simpler per-warp base computation.

shapes.description: cp.async-pipelined WMMA, single warp, double-buffered K-loop with prefetch/consume.
shapes.honest_notes: hexa is dramatically leaner (3.6x fewer SASS instructions). HONEST CAVEAT: this comparison is NOT apples-to-apples. hexa hand-emits raw cp.async.cg.shared.global with a thread-fanout pattern (each thread issues one 16B copy, single mbarrier-free wait_all). nvcc reference uses the canonical cuda::pipeline + cooperative_groups::memcpy_async API which compiles to a fuller pipeline_shared_state + producer_acquire/commit + consumer_wait/release state machine plus implicit mbarrier ops. Real-world parity would require nvcc using __pipeline_memcpy_async + __pipeline_commit + __pipeline_wait_prior intrinsics directly; that variant left as future work.

shapes.description: TF32 WMMA single-tile, M=16 N=16 K=8, single warp, explicit fp32->tf32 cvt.rna in fragment.
shapes.honest_notes: Identical SASS count. This is expected because hexa's step5_tf32.ptx was itself generated by nvcc 12.0 (see PTX header comment 'Generated by NVIDIA NVVM Compiler') -- so the nvcc reference effectively reproduces the same source.

shapes.description: 256x256 HGEMM, 4x4 thread-block grid (16 blocks), 16 warps per block, K-loop K_TILES=16.
shapes.honest_notes: hexa is 15.4% leaner. Note: this counts SASS per-kernel (not per-iter) -- both hexa and nvcc have the same K-loop structure but different unroll/address-folding strategies. Per-block work is identical (one 64x64 sub-block via 16 warp tiles). Consistent with step3 direction (hexa's PTX address-arithmetic folds more cleanly when ptxas->sass lowers).

summary.headline: hexa cookbook PTX is SASS-count competitive with nvcc CUDA C reference: 2 shapes equal, 3 leaner, 1 heavier (step2 multitile +17.6%). step4 cp.async hexa-vs-nvcc ratio (0.28) is misleading because nvcc reference uses higher-level cuda::pipeline API; mark non-comparable.
summary.refutes_stash1_claim: Stash 1 had claimed hexa step1=53.9% of nvcc SASS. This measurement (and a1f9d80b's step1 1.000 baseline) refutes that.  Across 5 directly-comparable shapes, hexa SASS ranges 0.808-1.176x nvcc, well within the band of two competent codegens.
